title: When a Project Initiation Document (PID) Can Be Omitted
summary: Describes the circumstances in which a full Project Initiation Document is unnecessary and a lightweight brief is sufficient, especially for very small, low‑risk work.
content: For very small, low‑risk initiatives (e.g., a single‑day design tweak) a lightweight brief may suffice. However, even in small projects, capturing at least context and deliverables can prevent misunderstand‑ings.
can_answer: When is it acceptable to skip a PID?
What minimal documentation is appropriate for tiny tasks?
examples: Updating a CSS color palette for an existing site – a short email brief can replace a full PID.
warnings: Even small tasks can benefit from a brief; skipping documentation entirely may cause loss of context.
